questions.question: Which of these is a living thing?
questions.hint: It grows and needs water.

questions.question: What covering does a bird have?
questions.hint: They help birds fly.

questions.question: Which part of a plant makes food using sunlight?
questions.hint: It is green and flat.

questions.question: A tadpole grows into which animal?
questions.hint: It lives on land and in water.

questions.question: Which animal is a mammal?
questions.hint: It breathes air and feeds its babies milk.

questions.question: What do roots do for a plant?
options: Absorb water and nutrients from soil
questions.hint: They grow underground.

questions.question: Which group do insects belong to?
questions.hint: They have 6 legs and an exoskeleton.

questions.question: What causes day and night?
questions.hint: The Earth is like a spinning top.

questions.question: What is the closest star to Earth?
questions.hint: We see it every day.

questions.question: What type of rock is formed from cooled lava?
questions.hint: It starts as very hot melted rock.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.hint: Its soil is red because of iron oxide.

questions.question: What is the water cycle step when water turns into vapour?
questions.hint: Heat from the Sun causes this.

questions.question: How long does it take Earth to orbit the Sun once?
questions.hint: It gives us all our seasons.

questions.question: What are clouds made of?
questions.hint: They fall as rain when they get heavy.

questions.question: Which object produces heat?
questions.hint: We use it to make toast hot.

questions.question: Sound travels best through which material?
questions.hint: The denser the material, the faster sound moves.

questions.question: What force pulls objects towards the ground?
questions.hint: Drop a ball and this brings it down.

questions.question: Which of these is a source of light?
questions.hint: It has batteries or a bulb to create light.

questions.question: If you push a toy car harder, how does it move?
questions.hint: More force means more speed.

questions.question: What does friction do between two surfaces?
questions.hint: Rub your hands together and feel the warmth.

questions.question: Which colour bends the most in a rainbow?
questions.hint: It is on the inside curve of the rainbow.

questions.question: What happens to ice when it gets hot?
questions.hint: Think about a snowman in summer.

questions.question: What is produced when wood burns?
questions.hint: You can see it rising from a campfire.

questions.question: Water boils and turns into which state?
questions.hint: You can see steam rising from a pot.

questions.question: Mixing vinegar and baking soda produces what?
questions.hint: You can see it fizzing and growing.

questions.question: Which of these dissolves in water?
questions.hint: You stir it into your tea.

questions.question: What are the three states of matter?
questions.hint: Ice is one example of each state.

questions.question: Which change is reversible?
questions.hint: You can just freeze it again.

questions.question: In a food chain, which organism is ALWAYS the starting point?
questions.hint: It uses sunlight to make its own food.

questions.question: What is the correct energy flow in a food chain?
questions.hint: Energy flows from plants to plant-eaters to meat-eaters.

questions.question: What causes the Moon to appear to change shape each night?
options: Earth casts a shadow on the Moon
The Moon turns its light on and off
We see different amounts of the sunlit Moon as it orbits Earth
The Sun moves closer to the Moon
questions.hint: The Moon shines by reflecting sunlight — our viewing angle changes.

questions.question: A ramp makes it easier to push a heavy box upward. What type of simple machine is a ramp?
questions.hint: It is a flat surface set at an angle.

questions.question: Melting chocolate is a physical change because...
options: It creates a completely new substance
It can be reversed by cooling back down
It cannot be changed back to solid
questions.hint: Can you turn the liquid chocolate back into solid again?

questions.question: What is the basic building block of all living things?
questions.hint: You need a microscope to see most of them.

questions.question: On the Great Barrier Reef, if coral bleaches and dies, which organisms are MOST directly harmed?
options: Fish that shelter and feed in coral
questions.hint: Many reef fish depend on coral for food and habitat.

questions.question: What causes coastal cliffs to gradually wear away over time?
questions.hint: Water breaks rock apart and carries the pieces away.

questions.question: When you rub a balloon on your hair and it sticks to a wall, which force is at work?
questions.hint: Rubbing transfers tiny charged particles between surfaces.

questions.question: Burning toast is irreversible. Which of these is ALSO an irreversible change?
questions.hint: Once your body breaks food down, can you get the original food back?

questions.question: Which human body system transports oxygen and nutrients around the body?
questions.hint: Your heart is the main organ of this system.

questions.question: In a food web, if all rabbits suddenly disappeared, the fox population would most likely...
questions.hint: Foxes rely on rabbits as a key food source.

questions.question: Earth's outer shell is broken into large slowly-moving pieces. What are these pieces called?
questions.hint: Their movement causes earthquakes and volcanoes at their boundaries.

questions.question: For a torch to light up, the electrical circuit must be...
questions.hint: Electricity must have a complete, unbroken path to flow.

questions.question: Lemon juice tastes sour and has a pH below 7. Substances like this are called...
questions.hint: Vinegar and lemon juice are both in this category.

questions.question: Which part of a cell contains DNA and controls the cell's activities?
questions.hint: It is often called the 'control centre' of the cell.

questions.question: During photosynthesis, plants use sunlight, water and carbon dioxide to produce glucose and...
questions.hint: This gas is released and we breathe it in to survive.

questions.question: Sedimentary rocks such as sandstone are formed by...
options: Layers of sediment being compressed over time
questions.hint: They often form in layers and may contain fossils.

questions.question: Newton's first law says an object at rest stays at rest UNLESS...
options: A net (unbalanced) force acts on it
questions.hint: Something must push or pull to change an object's motion.

questions.question: Water (H₂O) is a compound. This means it...
options: Is a mixture of different materials that can be separated easily
Contains only one type of atom
Is made of two or more elements chemically bonded together
Cannot be broken down by any means
questions.hint: H stands for hydrogen and O for oxygen — joined by chemical bonds.

questions.question: Which one of these is a CHEMICAL change (a new substance is formed)?
questions.hint: The iron reacts with oxygen and water to form a completely new substance — iron oxide.

questions.question: A ray of light bends as it passes from air into glass. This phenomenon is called...
questions.hint: This is why a straw appears bent when sitting in a glass of water.

questions.question: The platypus and echidna lay eggs yet feed their young milk. What group do these unique Australian animals belong to?
questions.hint: They are mammals, but share one very unusual trait with reptiles.

questions.question: Australia sits on the Indo-Australian tectonic plate. When two plates collide, what is a likely result?
options: Mountain ranges and volcanic activity forming
questions.hint: Plates crashing together push rock upward over millions of years.
